title: notopleura lateralis
intro: Notopleura lateralis, commonly known as Quina, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and it has been found to have potential in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and Alzheimer's disease. The therapeutic actions of Notopleura lateralis include its ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, reduce inflammation, and improve cognitive function. Its bioactive constituents include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Notopleura lateralis has many benefits, such as its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Its antimicrobial properties also make it effective in treating infections and wounds, while its antioxidant properties help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Additionally, Notopleura lateralis has been shown to have anti-cancer properties, reducing the risk of certain types of cancer, and its neuroprotective properties may help prevent or slow the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. By leveraging these benefits, individuals can enjoy improved physical and mental health, increased energy levels, and a better quality of life.

constituents: Notopleura lateralis has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The alkaloids present in this plant, including notopleurine and notoplurine, have been found to exhibit antimalarial and antihypertensive effects, thereby improving the lives of individuals suffering from these conditions by reducing the risk of malaria and alleviating hypertension-related symptoms. The flavonoids and phenolic compounds in Notopleura lateralis, such as quercetin and ferulic acid, have been found to have anti-cancer and anti-aging properties, which can help in preventing cancer and slowing down the aging process. By utilizing the medicinal constituents of Notopleura lateralis, individuals can lead healthier and more fulfilling lives, with improved physical and mental well-being.

side_effects: Notopleura lateralis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use or high doses may also lead to liver damage, kidney problems, and allergic reactions. In some cases, individuals may experience dizziness, headaches, or fatigue due to the plant's interaction with other medications or pre-existing health conditions. Improper use of Notopleura lateralis can also cause skin irritation, such as rashes or itching, and in severe cases, may lead to respiratory problems or cardiac issues.
